title: London System
shortDescription: A solid 1.d4 setup with Bf4, e3, Nf3, Bd3, and Nbd2, reaching a comparable structure against almost any Black defense.

root.comment: White claims the center with the queen's pawn.

children.comment: A flexible developing move that keeps several systems available.
The defining London move: develop the bishop before playing e3.
White builds a solid pawn chain and frees the dark-squared bishop.
The bishop points toward h7 and supports kingside attacking ideas.
White completes the core London setup.
The center is reinforced and b4 ideas become harder for Black.

line.name: Classical London Setup
line.description: The standard London move order with Bf4, e3, Nf3, Bd3 and Nbd2.

children.comment: A useful waiting move that preserves the bishop on g3.

line.name: Classical Setup with h3
line.description: White gives the bishop a retreat square and prevents ...Nh5 from gaining the bishop pair.

children.comment: The knight occupies its ideal London outpost.
White builds pressure on the kingside.

line.name: Basic Kingside Attack
line.description: A typical London attacking plan with Ne5, Qf3 and kingside pressure.

children.comment: White begins a direct kingside expansion.

line.name: Thematic h4 Attack
line.description: White expands on the kingside after completing development.

children.comment: White immediately creates threats around h7.

line.name: Ne5 and Kingside Pressure
line.description: A thematic London attack built around Ne5, Qf3 and h4.

line.name: Kingside Sacrifice on h7
line.description: White uses Qf3, Ne5 and h4 to build a direct attack.

children.comment: The classic bishop sacrifice begins.

line.name: Greek Gift Pattern
line.description: A model attacking pattern where Bxh7+ becomes possible after Black weakens the kingside.

line.name: London into a Carlsbad Structure
line.description: White exchanges on d5 and plays against the resulting queenside structure.

line.name: Minority-Style b4 Plan
line.description: White uses c3 and b4 to gain space and restrain Black's queenside.

children.comment: Black immediately attacks the center.
White reinforces d4 and prepares Nbd2.

line.name: Early ...c5: Solid c3 Setup
line.description: White supports d4 and keeps the familiar London structure.

children.comment: Now Qb3 is legal because the c-pawn has moved and the diagonal is open.

line.name: Queen-Trade Main Line
line.description: White accepts an early queen trade and relies on the sound London structure.

children.comment: White attacks d5 and can transpose toward Queen's Gambit structures.

line.name: London with c4
line.description: White temporarily leaves the pure system setup to challenge Black's center directly.

children.comment: Black pressures b2.
A simple way to defend b2 while keeping the pawn structure intact.

line.name: Early ...Qb6: Qc1 Defense
line.description: White protects b2 without abandoning the London setup.

children.comment: White protects b2 and prepares c4 or a queenside fianchetto.

line.name: Early ...Qb6: b3 Setup
line.description: White protects b2 with b3 and prepares a stable queenside structure.

line.name: Main Line: ...Qb6 and Qb3
line.description: White meets pressure on b2 by offering a queen trade.

line.name: Main Line with Nc3 and ...Qb6
line.description: White develops quickly and accepts a more tactical version of the London.

line.name: Early ...c5 with a Queenside Pin
line.description: White responds to ...Qb6 by using Nc3 and Nb5 to create tactical pressure.

children.comment: Black accepts the poisoned pawn.
White attacks the exposed queen and gains time for development.

line.name: Poisoned b2 Pawn
line.description: A sharp line where Black grabs b2 and White gains development and tactical chances.

line.name: Against the Slav Setup
line.description: White develops normally against ...c6 and ...d5.

children.comment: White attacks b7 and increases pressure on d5.

line.name: Slav Setup: Qb3 Pressure
line.description: White targets b7 after Black develops the light-squared bishop.

line.name: Sharp Slav-London Hybrid
line.description: White combines Bf4 with c4 and Qb3 pressure against Black's queenside.

children.comment: Black immediately questions the bishop.
The bishop stays active and creates an awkward pin.
The knight recaptures the advanced pawn and restores material balance.

line.name: Against Early ...Nh5
line.description: White preserves the London bishop or gives it up only under favorable conditions.

line.name: Mirror London: Early ...Bf5
line.description: White challenges Black's mirror setup and prepares c4 or Bd3.

children.comment: The Jobava move order creates immediate tactical ideas.

line.name: Jobava-London Transition
line.description: White uses Nc3 and Nb5 ideas, entering a more aggressive London structure.

line.name: Against the King's Indian Setup
line.description: White keeps the London structure against ...g6 and ...Bg7.

children.comment: White establishes a strong central pawn duo.

line.name: Anti-King's Indian e4 Expansion
line.description: White uses the London bishop outside the chain, then builds a broad center.

line.name: King's Indian Setup: c3 and e4 Plan
line.description: White prepares e4 while keeping the bishop outside the pawn chain.

line.name: Against a Grünfeld-Style Setup
line.description: White maintains the London structure while Black attacks the center with ...c5.

line.name: Against a Queen's Indian Style Setup
line.description: White completes the London shell before deciding on c4 or c3.

children.comment: White grabs queenside space and limits ...cxb4 ideas.

line.name: Queenside Expansion with b4
line.description: White gains space on the queenside when Black's setup is passive.

children.comment: White finally strikes in the center.

line.name: Central Break with e4
line.description: White prepares the thematic e4 break after completing development.

line.name: Against the Dutch Defense
line.description: White uses the London bishop to challenge Black's kingside setup.

line.name: Against the Dutch Stonewall
line.description: White exchanges the dangerous dark-squared bishop and targets weakened squares.

children.comment: White challenges Black's kingside structure through the center.

line.name: Against the Leningrad Dutch
line.description: White keeps the London setup and attacks the weakened dark squares.
